papers.title: Subtime: Reversible Information Exchange and the Emergence of Classical Time
papers.abstract: We formalize the concept of subtime -- a reversible mode of information interchange within entangled systems -- and show how classical time emerges as an asymptotic limit through decoherence. Building on the photon clock model, in which a single photon confined between two ideal mirrors creates an alternating causality regime, we develop a process-theoretic framework for understanding time emergence.

papers.title: Machine learning the arrow of time in solid-state spins
papers.abstract: Understanding the emergence of the thermodynamic arrow of time in quantum systems through machine learning approaches applied to solid-state spin systems.

papers.title: The Semantic Arrow of Time, Part V: The Leibniz Bridge -- Toward a Unified Theory of Semantic Time
papers.abstract: This is the final paper in the five-part series The Semantic Arrow of Time, presenting a unified theory of semantic time through the Leibniz Bridge framework.

papers.title: The Causal Second Law
papers.abstract: Under certain key assumptions that are familiar from physicalist accounts of the special sciences and from physics, causal regularities have an associated notion of entropy, establishing a causal second law.

papers.title: Arrow of time problem in gravitational collapse
papers.abstract: We investigate the arrow of time problem in the context of gravitational collapse, examining the relationship between thermodynamic and gravitational arrows.

papers.title: Multidimensional arrow of time
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the influence of extra dimensions on the nature of the arrow of time, proposing a multidimensional framework.

papers.title: Measuring unconventional causal structures in monitored dynamics
papers.abstract: In systems with measurement and post-selection, causal influence can occur between spacelike separated regions. We study the causal structure and emergent arrow of time.

papers.title: Baryogenesis from the Thermodynamic Arrow of Time: a Transfer-Function Bound and an Entropy-Clock Mechanism
papers.abstract: We propose an entropy clock mechanism tied to thermodynamic irreversibility that generates baryon asymmetry from the thermodynamic arrow of time.

papers.title: Entropy and the arrow of time in population dynamics
papers.abstract: We explore entropy formulas for stochastic processes with absorbing states in population genetics models, considering the Moran process as a paradigm.

papers.title: Pre-bangian origin of our entropy and time arrow
papers.abstract: In string cosmology, classical and quantum effects generate the correct amount of entropy to saturate a Hubble entropy bound and identify the arrow of time within our Universe.

keyInsights: Recent work on subtime formalizes reversible information exchange and classical time emergence through decoherence
Machine learning approaches applied to quantum systems for arrow of time detection
Semantic arrow of time series completed with unified Leibniz Bridge theory
Causal second law establishes entropy connection to causal regularities
Gravitational collapse continues to show tension between thermodynamic and gravitational arrows
Multidimensional frameworks propose extra dimensions influence time arrow nature
Entropy-clock mechanisms link thermodynamic irreversibility to cosmological phenomena
chronosRelevance: 直接支持时间涌现论研究，特别是亚时间 (subtime) 概念与 ITLCT 的信息场理论高度相关；熵钟机制为热力学选择原理提供新证据；多维时间箭头框架支持时间的涌现性和相对性
